title: Wandering Souls - a solo RPG / Adventure / Journaling game
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oCAyG5S7cIw

Daniel (@dungeondive) previews Wandering Souls, a solo journaling RPG by Cody Barr of Sleepy Sasquatch Games (of Glaive fame), with art by friend-of-the-channel perplexing ruins. It missed its Kickstarter goal but is available on itch, and Daniel printed his own zine to play this early, still-being-finalised draft — which he stresses, because a few rules remain rough (the difference between "max" and "current" stats, and the flee-damage rules, are unclear).

You play a Wanderer braving a shrouded realm beset by Shadows. Mechanically it leans on 2d6-plus-stat rolls against target numbers, with a standard deck of cards (jokers as Shadows) driving events, locations, beings, treasure and threats. Quests resolve by accumulating "track" or "lore" points; characters take wounds and Shadow points until they retire — at which point a rogue-legacy system folds them into the world for future characters to benefit from or be cursed by. Spirit doubles as both a stat and a re-roll currency.

Daniel walks through a live quest: protecting a wizard's exorcism, fighting off wolf-riders, picking up a rhyming bard, and fleeing a tough Shadow Rider. He likes the card-as-oracle approach and the solid mechanical backbone beneath the journaling, and encourages viewers to send Cody feedback.

----

When a game is still in preview and a little rough, do you enjoy getting in early to shape it, or do you wait for the finished version?
